steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question tests Boundaries. The blank falls between two independent clauses: “the guide soon grew in scope and ____” and “its modest French eateries were replaced with some of the most esteemed restaurants from around the world.” The decisive clue is that both sides can stand alone, so a comma + coordinating conjunction is required to join them correctly.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘influence’: No punctuation or conjunction separates the two independent clauses — this creates a run-on (fused sentence). ✗
  • Choice B — ‘influence, and’: The comma closes the first independent clause and and (a coordinating conjunction) introduces the second — the standard FANBOYS rule for joining two independent clauses is satisfied. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘influence,’: A comma alone cannot join two independent clauses; this is a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘influence and’: The coordinating conjunction is present but the required comma before it is missing, violating the comma + FANBOYS rule. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
